chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Sometimes we have a class, for example, we have an employee class

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: and that class defines the employee, what is his employee ID, what is his main role, what is the name of the company.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Now assume that I want to use this class to make a new class, Programmer,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: or I want to use this class to make another class, Manager.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: In the manager class, the basic methods and properties of the employee class will remain,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: but I want to add some more things with it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: For example, I will add in the programmer that which programming language does the person use,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: this kind of information.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So how can I extend my existing class, which I have made with a lot of effort,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: the class named Employee, and make a new class, Programmer or Manager.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: In today's video, I will tell you this.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Let's go to the computer screen and let's see all of this in code.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: The concept I am going to tell you today is a very good concept of object-oriented programming.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And you should definitely know that concept, and that is the concept of inheritance.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: If you want to become a successful Python developer, then you should definitely know this concept.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So let's start this concept without any delay.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Assume I wrote a class here and I said that I want to make a class named Employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I worked hard and said that I want the name of the employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I said that I want the name of the employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And I want the name and generate an ID of it and give it to me.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: There can be a lot of details about it, but keeping things simple, I will say that

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: self.name is equal to name set and self.id is equal to id set.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I made some utility methods, like I made a method named showDetails.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I made a method and I wanted to show the employee's details in it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So I simply printed an f-string and said that the name of employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And here I will put its id, self.id and I will write self.name.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: This is a very simple thing, I can make a new employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Assume what is the name of this employee?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Assume the name of this employee is Rohan Das.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Assume the name of this employee is Rohan Das.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And now what I am doing is, this is Rohan Das's name and I am giving him an id of 420.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: This is the id that was generated automatically by using a system.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: This is generated, sorry Rohan Das.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Now if I run this, then what will happen?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Nothing will happen, it will become an object, but if I run showDetails on it,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: then definitely a lot will happen.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So if I run this, then the details will show me that the name of employee 420 is Rohan Das.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I will fix this a little bit.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What id is this generating? I will make it 400.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: 400, okay.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So here see, the name of employee 400 is Rohan Das.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Can I make 50-60 employees like this?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So if I want, I can make 50-60 employees here, there is no doubt about it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: You don't even have to be, I can make it by putting e1, e2, e3, e4.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I can also make a list of them.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Here I will make it e2.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Here I will make it Harry.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I will make it 4100 and run it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So you see here, employee.showDetails, employee.showDetails, by doing this,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Rohan Das's and Harry's details will come here.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Okay, so no surprises there.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Now assume that this class is made.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What is this class? It is made.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And this class is used.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What is read? It is used.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: It is made, it is used.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Now someone says that I have to change this class and make it a programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So whoever was using it, they will say that we have made it a code base with the name of employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What is this joke of 8000-9000 lines?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So would you like to change it?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: You will think that let's change it, let's add it to this.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: They can't do it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: There will be a problem if we do it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What will be the problem?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: The biggest problem will be that if I change this class,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: then wherever this class is being used, there will be an error.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And I don't want such an error to come.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Why would I want an error? I don't want an error.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So here class employee and if I rename it as class programmer,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: then there will be a big problem.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So rename is not an option.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Do we have a better option?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Yes, there is. And that is inheritance.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So what can I do here?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I can write class programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: After writing programmer, I can write employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: This means that I have made a new class called programmer,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: which has everything that is in the employee class.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And there are all these things that I am going to write.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So what will I do?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I am going to write here, def show language.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And let's say I write self here.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And after this, I print here.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: The default language is Python.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Python. Let's say I write something like this.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: The default language is Python.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So can I run show language on the employee?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Definitely not.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: I will run it and show you.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: If I do e2.showlanguage, then it will not work.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Why won't it work?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Because show language is not defined on the employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So as you can see, there is an error here.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Employee object has no attribute show language.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And why doesn't it come?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: If I was a compiler, I would have thrown it too.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So see here that e2.showlanguage is showing an error.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: But if I make e2 a programmer here, what will happen?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So here you can see that this program has run.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Because I changed it to programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So we can only use the same method that is in that class.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: But here because I have made the programmer from the employee class.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Just like a father's son is a son,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Similarly, the programmer's father is an employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So he has all the qualities of a father.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And he also has his own qualities.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So show language has its own method.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Because his father doesn't know.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Like today's children know how to use phones, laptops, etc.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Maybe their father doesn't know that much.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So he learned everything from his father.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: But he also learned some things from himself.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Like show language is with the programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: But his father is not with the employee.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So in a way you can understand that

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: All the methods and properties of the employee have come to the programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Along with that, we can extend it and put more things in it.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Now there are many types of inheritance.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Like single inheritance, multiple inheritance, multi-level inheritance,

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Hierarchical inheritance and hybrid inheritance.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: What are all these?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: We will see in the coming videos.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: And I will show you with an example.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: But for now, you just practice it and make some classes.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Can we make another class again and make a new class from the programmer?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: This is exactly the same question that can a person's child be a child?

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Obviously it can be.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: Because if you become a programmer from an employee, then you can become something from a programmer.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So there is no doubt in that.

chunks.title: Inheritance in Python
chunks.text: So that's all for now.
